name: TAO Delayed Retest Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: delayed entry after breakout retest holds
thesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing delayed retest continuation: enter only after the breakout survives a retest, not on the first impulse. The edge should come from better trade quality rather than maximum signal count.
invalidation_condition: Abort if retest-hold continuation remains too sparse, if TAO only prints impulsive spikes with no orderly retest, or if held retests fail to extend after entry.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A breakout occurs, price revisits the break zone without losing acceptance, and continuation entry is taken on resumed directional movement rather than the original breakout bar.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Delayed retests can filter false breaks by letting weak impulses fail before entry. In a chop/transition mix, that may preserve the few real breakouts while avoiding many fake first bars.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is useful because it stress-tests whether the retest grammar can survive on a high-volatility structurally different asset.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If TAO only gaps through levels or mean reverts too hard, orderly retests will not appear often enough.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If retests hold but second legs are short, reduce runner ambition before tightening the retest logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Lower signal count than the reversion idea, but better average trade quality when continuation actually emerges.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO plus one cleaner lane show tradable retest holds with enough density and post-cost expansion.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, chaotic spikes with no retest structure, or repeated holds that fail to extend.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad retest-hold conditions instead of exact candle timing

validation_basket.why_included: Stress-test lane for whether delayed retest continuation survives on a volatile structurally different asset.
validation_basket.continue_if: Retest holds appear with enough density and meaningful continuation.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: No orderly retest structure appears or density collapses.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: A small number of high-quality retest holds should outperform immediate breakout chasing.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same delayed-entry continuation logic works on a cleaner expressive lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: SOL shows comparable retest-hold behavior with better structure.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner but still meaningful retest continuations.

validation_basket.why_included: Determines whether the retest grammar generalizes to a cleaner market.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH still prints delayed retests with orderly follow-through.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Only the wild lane shows the pattern.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner continuation legs after held retests.
